AccessibilityVerbosity.description: Verbosity level for screen reader announcements.

oneOf.description: Default verbosity level, includes help string.
Concise level, only announces `value` from AccessibilityContent.

AgentModeCodingPermissionsType.description: File read permission level for the agent.

oneOf.description: Agent Mode must ask for explicit permission for any type of file read.
Agent Mode can always read files without explicit consent.

Agent Mode can only read certain files without explicit consent.

The specific filepaths are backed by the
[`AISettings::agent_mode_coding_file_read_allowlist`] setting.
Use a custom arrangement of toolbar items.

AgentToolbarItemKind.description: An item that can appear in the agent toolbar.

AltScreenPaddingMode.description: How padding is applied in full-screen terminal apps.

oneOf.description: Use the same padding as the block list.
Use a custom uniform padding value.

AppIcon.description: The app icon displayed in the dock.

oneOf.description: Use a custom arrangement of toolbar items.
A chip that shows the plan and todo list for the current conversation.

CustomSecretRegex.description: A custom regex pattern for detecting and redacting secrets.

name.description: Optional display name for this secret pattern.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match secrets.

name.description: The display name of the custom theme.

path.description: The file path to the custom theme definition.

oneOf.description: New sessions start in the terminal mode (default).
New sessions start in agent view.
New sessions start in cloud (ambient) agent mode.

New sessions open a user-defined tab config.
The specific config is identified by the companion `default_tab_config_path` setting.

New sessions open in a local Docker sandbox.
Requires the `LocalDockerSandbox` feature flag; falls back to `Terminal` when disabled.

DisplayIdx.description: Which display to use when multiple monitors are connected.

oneOf.description: An external display, identified by index.

DriveSortOrder.description: Sort order for Warp Drive items.

oneOf.description: Sort by newest revision first in main index, most recently trashed in trash index
Sort by object type, with folders first

EditorChoice.description: Which editor to use when opening files.

EditorLayout.description: Layout used when opening files in the editor.

EnforceMinimumContrast.description: When to adjust foreground color to ensure readability against the background.

oneOf.description: FG color can be changed, but only if the FG is specified with default colors
FG color is changed regardless of how FG was specified

ExtraMetaKeys.description: Additional keys that act as the meta key.

left_alt.description: Whether the left Alt key acts as meta.

right_alt.description: Whether the right Alt key acts as meta.

GraphicsBackend.description: Graphics rendering backend used for display output.

HeaderToolbarChipSelection.description: Configuration for the header toolbar chips in the vertical tab panel header.

HeaderToolbarItemKind.description:
A configurable item in the vertical tabs header toolbar.

Each variant represents a panel toggle button that can be placed on either
the left or right side of the toolbar. The side determines which side of the
main content area the panel opens on.

InputMode.description: Direction that blocks flow in the terminal viewport.

oneOf.description:
The most recent blocks are at the bottom of the screen and new blocks
are added at the bottom as the blocklist grows

The most recent blocks are at the top of the screen and new blocks
are added at the top as the blocklist grows
The input starts at the top and gets pushed down by commands above it.

NewSessionShell.description: Shell to use when opening new sessions.

oneOf.description: Use the operating system's default shell.
A Windows Subsystem for Linux distribution.

NewTabPlacement.description: Where new tabs are placed in the tab bar.

NotificationsMode.description: Whether the user has enabled or disabled notifications.

oneOf.description: Notifications have not been configured yet.
The notifications banner has been dismissed.

is_agent_task_completed_enabled.description: Whether to notify when an agent task completes.

is_long_running_enabled.description: Whether to notify when a long-running command completes.

is_needs_attention_enabled.description: Whether to notify when a session needs attention.

is_password_prompt_enabled.description: Whether to notify when a password prompt is detected.

long_running_threshold.description: Threshold in seconds for long-running command notifications.

mode.description: Whether notifications are enabled, disabled, or not yet configured.

play_notification_sound.description: Whether to play a sound with notifications.

QuakeModePinPosition.description: Screen edge to pin the hotkey window to.

active_pin_position.description: Screen edge where the hotkey window is pinned.

hide_window_when_unfocused.description: Whether to hide the hotkey window when it loses focus.

keybinding.description: Keyboard shortcut to toggle the hotkey window. Format: modifiers (cmd, ctrl, alt, shift, meta) and a key joined by '-', e.g. "cmd-shift-a" or "alt-enter". Bindings are case-sensitive: when shift is present, the key must be its shifted form (e.g., "ctrl-shift-E", not "ctrl-shift-e").

pin_position_to_size_percentages.description: Window size percentages for each pin position.

pin_screen.description: Display to pin the hotkey window to.

SecretDisplayMode.description: How detected secrets are visually displayed.

oneOf.description: Show secrets with gray color and strikethrough styling
Show secrets normally with no visual treatment (but are still detected/redacted)

SelectedSystemThemes.description: Themes to use when following the system light/dark mode.

dark.description: The theme to use in dark mode.

light.description: The theme to use in light mode.

SizePercentages.description: Window size as width and height percentages of the screen.

height.description: Height as a percentage of screen height (0–100).

width.description: Width as a percentage of screen width (0–100).

oneOf.description: Always prompt the user before installing (default).
Automatically install and connect without prompting.
Never install; fall back to legacy warpification.

StartupShell.description: Shell to start terminal sessions with. Use null for the system default, or one of "bash", "zsh", "fish", "pwsh", or a custom shell command/path.

TabCloseButtonPosition.description: Position of the close button on tabs.

oneOf.description: A user-provided custom theme loaded from a file.
A custom theme using the Base16 color scheme format.

ThinStrokes.description: When to render text with thinner strokes for a lighter appearance.

oneOf.description: Never render glyphs using thin strokes.
Render glyphs using thin strokes when rendering on a low-DPI display.
Render glyphs using thin strokes when rendering on a high-DPI display.
Always render glyphs using thin strokes.

ThinkingDisplayMode.description: Controls how agent thinking is displayed after streaming.

oneOf.description: Show reasoning blocks while streaming, then collapse them when complete (default).
Always keep reasoning blocks expanded, even after streaming finishes.

UserNativePreference.description: Preference for using the native desktop app or the web app.

VerticalTabsCompactSubtitle.description: Subtitle shown on compact vertical tabs.

VerticalTabsDisplayGranularity.description: Granularity of rows displayed in the vertical tabs panel.

VerticalTabsPrimaryInfo.description: Primary information displayed on vertical tabs.

VerticalTabsTabItemMode.description: Tab item display mode in vertical tabs.

VerticalTabsViewMode.description: Display mode for the vertical tab bar.

VoiceInputToggleKey.description: Physical key used to toggle voice input.

oneOf.description: Super, Windows, or Command key (left side).
Super, Windows, or Command key (right side).

WorkingDirectoryConfig.description: Configuration for the initial working directory of new sessions.

advanced_mode.description: Whether to use separate settings per session source.

global.description: Default working directory settings used when advanced mode is off.

new_tab.description: Working directory settings for new tab sessions.

new_window.description: Working directory settings for new window sessions.

split_pane.description: Working directory settings for split pane sessions.

oneOf.description: Start a new session in the user's home directory.
Start a new session in the same directory as the previous session.
Start a new session in a specific directory.

WorkingDirectoryPerSourceConfig.description: Working directory settings for a specific session source.

custom_dir.description: Custom directory path, used when mode is CustomDir.

mode.description: How the working directory is determined.

WorkspaceDecorationVisibility.description: When workspace decorations such as the tab bar are visible.

oneOf.description: Only show workspace decorations on hover.

description: JSON Schema for Warp settings (oss channel, 190 settings)

accessibility_verbosity.description: The verbosity level for screen reader announcements.

is_settings_sync_enabled.description: Whether settings are synced across devices via the cloud.

cloud_conversation_storage_enabled.description: Whether conversations are stored in the cloud.

rules_enabled.description: Whether the agent uses your saved rules during requests.

warp_drive_context_enabled.description: Whether Warp Drive context is included in AI requests.

file_based_mcp_enabled.description: Whether third-party file-based MCP servers are automatically detected.

agent_mode_coding_file_read_allowlist.description: File paths the agent can read without asking for permission.

agent_mode_coding_permissions.description: The file read permission level for the agent.

agent_mode_command_execution_allowlist.description: Commands that the agent can execute without explicit permission.

agent_mode_command_execution_denylist.description: Commands that the agent must always ask before executing.

agent_mode_execute_readonly_commands.description: Whether the agent can auto-execute read-only commands without asking.

auto_dismiss_composer_after_submit.description: Whether CLI agent Rich Input automatically closes after the user submits a prompt.

auto_open_composer_on_cli_agent_start.description: Whether CLI agent Rich Input automatically opens when a CLI agent session starts.

auto_toggle_composer.description: Whether CLI agent Rich Input automatically closes and reopens based on the agent's blocked state.

cli_agent_toolbar_chip_selection_setting.description: Controls the layout of context chips in the CLI Agent toolbar.

cli_agent_toolbar_enabled_commands.description: Maps custom toolbar command patterns to specific CLI agents.

should_render_cli_agent_toolbar.description: Whether to show the CLI agent footer for coding agent commands.

voice_input_enabled.description: Controls whether voice input is enabled for AI interactions.

voice_input_toggle_key.description: The key used to toggle voice input.

agent_mode_query_suggestions_enabled.description: Controls whether prompt suggestions are shown in agent mode.

code_suggestions_enabled.description: Controls whether AI code suggestions are enabled.

enabled.description: Controls whether proactive AI features like suggestions are enabled.

git_operations_autogen_enabled.description: Controls whether AI auto-generates commit messages and PR title/body in the code review dialogs.

intelligent_autosuggestions_enabled.description: Controls whether AI-powered intelligent autosuggestions are enabled.

shared_block_title_generation_enabled.description: Controls whether titles are auto-generated when sharing blocks.

agent_toolbar_chip_selection_setting.description: Controls the layout of context chips in the Agent Mode toolbar.

ai_auto_detection_enabled.description: Controls whether AI automatically detects natural language input.

ai_command_denylist.description: Commands to exclude from AI natural language autodetection.

include_agent_commands_in_history.description: Whether agent-executed commands are included in command history.

nld_in_terminal_enabled.description: Controls whether natural language detection is enabled in the terminal input.

show_agent_tips.description: Whether agent tips are displayed in the input.

show_model_selectors_in_prompt.description: Whether to show AI model selectors in the input prompt.

is_any_ai_enabled.description: Controls whether all AI features are enabled.

agent_attribution_enabled.description: Whether the Warp Agent adds an attribution co-author line to commit messages and pull requests it creates.

auto_handoff_on_sleep_enabled.description: Whether Warp automatically hands off local agent conversations to cloud when the computer is about to sleep.

cloud_agent_computer_use_enabled.description: Whether computer use is enabled for cloud agent conversations.

feedback_bundled_skill_enabled.description: Whether Warp's built-in feedback skill is available to the Warp Agent.

open_conversation_layout_preference.description: Whether to open agent conversations in a new tab or a split pane.

should_force_disable_ampersand_handoff.description: Whether to force-disable the & prefix for cloud handoff compose mode.

should_render_use_agent_toolbar_for_user.description: Whether to show the "Use Agent" footer for terminal commands.

should_show_oz_updates_in_zero_state.description: Whether the "What's new" section is shown in the agent view.

show_conversation_history.description: Whether conversation history appears in the tools panel.

thinking_display_mode.description: Controls how agent thinking traces are displayed after streaming.

should_show_bootstrap_block.description: Whether the bootstrap block is visible in the terminal.

should_show_in_band_command_blocks.description: Whether in-band command blocks are visible in the terminal.

should_show_ssh_block.description: Whether the SSH connection block is visible in the terminal.

show_block_dividers.description: Whether to show dividers between terminal blocks.

show_jump_to_bottom_of_block_button.description: Whether to show the jump-to-bottom button in long command output.

cursor_display_type.description: The visual style of the cursor.

alt_screen_padding.description: Controls padding around full-screen terminal applications.

app_icon.description: The app icon displayed in the dock.

input_mode.description: The position of the terminal input.

focus_pane_on_hover.description: Whether panes are focused when hovered over.

should_dim_inactive_panes.description: Whether inactive panes are visually dimmed.

spacing.description: Controls the spacing between terminal blocks.

header_toolbar_chip_selection.description: Configuration for the header toolbar chips in the vertical tab panel header.

preserve_active_tab_color.description: Whether to preserve the active tab's color when switching tabs.

show_indicators_button.description: Whether to show activity indicators on tabs.

tab_close_button_position.description: Position of the close button on tabs.

workspace_decoration_visibility.description: When workspace decorations such as the tab bar are visible.

ai_font_name.description: The font used for AI-generated content.

enforce_minimum_contrast.description: Whether to enforce minimum contrast for text readability.

font_name.description: The monospace font used in the terminal.

font_size.description: The size of the monospace font in the terminal.

font_weight.description: The weight of the monospace font in the terminal.

ligature_rendering_enabled.description: Whether to render font ligatures in the terminal.

line_height_ratio.description: The line height ratio for terminal text.

match_ai_font.description: Whether the AI font automatically matches the terminal font.

match_notebook_to_monospace_font_size.description: Whether the notebook font size matches the terminal font size.

notebook_font_size.description: The font size used in notebooks.

use_thin_strokes.description: Whether to use thin font strokes on macOS.

selected_system_themes.description: The themes to use for system light and dark modes.

system_theme.description: Whether to match the system light/dark theme.

compact_subtitle.description: Subtitle shown on compact vertical tabs.

display_granularity.description: Granularity of rows displayed in the vertical tabs panel.

enabled.description: Whether to display tabs vertically instead of horizontally.

primary_info.description: The primary information displayed on vertical tabs.

show_details_on_hover.description: Whether to show a details sidecar when hovering over a vertical tab.

show_diff_stats.description: Whether to show diff stats on vertical tabs.

show_panel_in_restored_windows.description: When restoring a window, open the vertical tabs panel even if it was closed when the session was saved.

show_pr_link.description: Whether to show PR links on vertical tabs.

tab_item_mode.description: Tab item display mode in vertical tabs.

use_latest_prompt_as_title.description: Whether vertical tab names for agent conversations use the latest user prompt.

view_mode.description: Display mode for the vertical tab bar.

left_panel_visibility_across_tabs.description: Whether the left panel visibility is shared across all tabs.

new_windows_num_columns.description: The number of columns for new windows when using a custom size.

new_windows_num_rows.description: The number of rows for new windows when using a custom size.

open_windows_at_custom_size.description: Whether to open new windows at a custom size instead of the default.

override_blur.description: The blur radius applied to the window background.

override_blur_texture.description: Whether to apply a blur texture to the window background.

override_opacity.description: The opacity of the window background, from 1 to 100 percent.

zoom_level.description: The zoom level for the window, as a percentage.

aws_bedrock_auth_refresh_command.description: The command to run to refresh AWS credentials for Bedrock.

aws_bedrock_auto_login.description: Whether to automatically run the AWS login command when Bedrock credentials expire.

aws_bedrock_credentials_enabled.description: Whether Warp should use your local AWS credentials for Bedrock-enabled requests.

aws_bedrock_profile.description: The AWS profile name to use for Bedrock credentials.

can_use_warp_credits_with_byok.description: Whether Warp credits can be used as a fallback for user-provided models.

auto_open_code_review_pane_on_first_agen.description: Whether to automatically open the code review pane when the agent makes its first change.

open_code_panels_file_editor.description: The editor used to open files from code panels.

open_file_editor.description: The editor used to open files.

open_file_layout.description: The layout used when opening files in the editor.

prefer_markdown_viewer.description: Whether to use the Markdown viewer when opening Markdown files.

prefer_tabbed_editor_view.description: Whether to prefer opening files in a tabbed editor view.

show_code_review_button.description: Whether to show the code review button on tabs.

show_code_review_diff_stats.description: Whether to show lines added/removed counts on the code review button.

show_global_search.description: Whether global file search is shown in the tools panel.

show_project_explorer.description: Whether the project explorer is shown in the tools panel.

use_warp_as_default_editor.description: Whether Warp is used as the default code editor.

agent_mode_codebase_context.description: Whether codebase context is provided to the AI agent.

agent_mode_codebase_context_auto_indexin.description: Whether automatic codebase indexing is enabled.

default_session_mode.description: The default mode for new terminal sessions.

link_tooltip.description: Whether to show a tooltip when hovering over links.

login_item.description: Whether to launch Warp automatically when you log in.

mouse_scroll_multiplier.description: The scroll speed multiplier for mouse scroll events.

new_tab_placement.description: Where new tabs are placed in the tab bar.

quit_on_last_window_closed.description: Whether to quit Warp when the last window is closed.

restore_session.description: Whether to restore the previous session when Warp starts up.

should_confirm_close_session.description: Whether to show a confirmation dialog when closing a session.

show_changelog_after_update.description: Whether the changelog is shown after an update.

show_warning_before_quitting.description: Whether to show a warning dialog before quitting Warp.

enabled.description: Whether the undo close feature is enabled.

grace_period.description: How long after closing a tab you can still undo the close.

user_native_preference.description: Whether to prefer the native desktop app or the web app.

enabled.description: Whether the dedicated hotkey window is enabled. Mutually exclusive with `global_hotkey.toggle_all_windows.enabled`; only one should be true at a time.

settings.description: Configuration options for Quake Mode window behavior.

enabled.description: Whether the hotkey that toggles visibility of all windows is enabled. Mutually exclusive with `global_hotkey.dedicated_window.enabled`; only one should be true at a time.

keybinding.description: The keybinding used for the global activation hotkey. Format: modifiers (cmd, ctrl, alt, shift, meta) and a key joined by '-', e.g. "cmd-shift-a" or "alt-enter". Bindings are case-sensitive: when shift is present, the key must be its shifted form (e.g., "ctrl-shift-E", not "ctrl-shift-e").

toast_duration_secs.description: How long notification toasts are displayed, in seconds.

custom_secret_regex_list.description: Custom regex patterns for detecting and redacting secrets.

enabled.description: Whether secret redaction is enabled to detect and obscure secrets in terminal output.

hide_secrets_in_block_list.description: Whether to hide detected secrets in the block list using asterisks.

secret_display_mode_setting.description: Controls how detected secrets are visually displayed in the terminal.

telemetry_enabled.description: Whether anonymous usage telemetry is collected.

new_session_shell_override.description: The shell to use when opening a new session.

startup_shell_override.description: The shell to use when Warp starts up.

working_directory_config.description: Controls the working directory used when opening new sessions.

force_x11.description: Whether to force X11 instead of Wayland on Linux.

linux_selection_clipboard.description: Whether the Linux primary selection clipboard is used.

prefer_low_power_gpu.description: Whether to prefer the integrated (low-power) GPU.

preferred_graphics_backend.description: The preferred graphics backend on Windows.

copy_on_select.description: Whether text is automatically copied to the clipboard when selected.

focus_reporting_enabled.description: Whether to forward focus and blur events to full-screen terminal applications.

alias_expansion_enabled.description: Whether shell alias expansion is enabled in the input.

at_context_menu_in_terminal_mode.description: Whether the @ context menu is available in terminal mode.

keybinding_hint.description: Whether autosuggestion keybinding hints are displayed.

show_ignore_button.description: Whether the ignore button is shown for autosuggestions.

classic_completions_mode.description: Whether classic completions mode is enabled.

command_corrections.description: Whether command corrections are suggested for mistyped commands.

completions_open_while_typing.description: Whether the completions menu opens automatically while typing.

enable_slash_commands_in_terminal.description: Whether slash commands are available in the terminal input.

error_underlining_enabled.description: Whether command errors are underlined in the input.

extra_meta_keys.description: Controls which additional keys are treated as meta keys.

honor_ps1.description: Whether to use your shell's PS1 prompt instead of the Warp prompt.

middle_click_paste_enabled.description: Whether middle-click pastes from the clipboard.

outline_codebase_symbols_for_at_context_.description: Whether codebase symbols appear in the @ context menu.

show_hint_text.description: Whether hint text is shown in the terminal input.

show_terminal_input_message_bar.description: Whether the terminal input message bar is shown.

syntax_highlighting.description: Whether syntax highlighting is enabled in the terminal input.

maximum_grid_size.description: The maximum number of rows in the terminal grid.

mouse_reporting_enabled.description: Whether to forward mouse events to full-screen terminal applications.

scroll_reporting_enabled.description: Whether to forward scroll events to full-screen terminal applications.

show_terminal_zero_state_block.description: Whether to show the AI zero-state block in new terminal sessions.

enabled.description: Whether double-click smart selection is enabled for URLs, emails, file paths, and identifiers.

word_char_allowlist.description: Characters that are considered part of a word for double-click selection when smart select is disabled.

use_audible_bell.description: Whether to play an audible bell sound on terminal bell events.

autocomplete_symbols.description: Whether matching symbols like brackets and quotes are auto-completed.

vim_status_bar.description: Whether the Vim status bar is displayed.

vim_unnamed_system_clipboard.description: Whether the Vim unnamed register uses the system clipboard.

sorting_choice.description: The sort order for items in Warp Drive.

enable_legacy_ssh_wrapper.description: Whether the legacy SSH wrapper is enabled for SSH sessions.

enable_ssh_warpification.description: Whether to enable Warp features in SSH sessions.

ssh_hosts_denylist.description: SSH hosts that should not trigger the warpification prompt.

use_ssh_tmux_wrapper.description: Whether to use a tmux-based wrapper for SSH warpification.

added_subshell_commands.description: Additional regex patterns for commands that should be recognized as subshells.

subshell_commands_denylist.description: Commands that should not trigger the subshell warpification prompt.

show_global_workflows_in_universal_searc.description: Whether to show global workflows in universal search results.

title: Warp Settings
